title: IT user support technicians
description: Analyze, test, troubleshoot, and evaluate existing network systems, such as local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), cloud networks, servers, and other data communications networks. Perform network maintenance to ensure networks operate correctly with minimal interruption. Provide technical assistance to computer users. Answer questions or resolve computer problems for clients in person, via telephone, or electronically. May provide assistance concerning the use of computer hardware and software, including printing, installation, word processing, electronic mail, and operating systems. All computer occupations not listed separately. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Install and configure networking equipment, including wireless routers, and ensure integration with existing systems. Perform routine maintenance or repairs on networking components or equipment.

Analyze and report computer network security breaches or attempted breaches, and troubleshoot security and network problems. Configure security settings or access permissions for networks and individuals, and recommend enhancements to improve security.

Configure and define parameters for installation or testing of LAN and WAN equipment, and monitor network performance data to ensure sufficient availability and identify issues for disaster recovery purposes.
Document network support activities, maintain logs of network activity, and create or update technical documentation for installations or changes to existing systems.
Provide technical assistance to computer users, answering inquiries regarding hardware or software operations and resolving problems via telephone, in person, or electronically.
Install network software, including security and firewall applications, and run monthly network reports to evaluate performance.
Create or revise user instructions and training materials related to network applications, and train users in the proper use of hardware and software systems.
Identify the causes of networking problems using diagnostic software, and perform testing of computer hardware and software to ensure proper operation and integration.
Evaluate local area network (LAN) or wide area network (WAN) performance data, and back up network data to ensure reliability and mitigate losses.
Research hardware or software products to meet networking or security needs and confer with staff and management to establish requirements for new systems or modifications.
Inspect equipment and prepare for delivery to users, ensuring proper installation of cables and software.
Conduct vulnerability assessments of information security systems, monitor networks for breaches, and contribute to developing best practices for information security.
Document help desk requests and resolutions, and maintain records of daily communication transactions, problems, and remedial actions taken.
Develop and execute tests that evaluate the performance and security of networks, including vulnerability assessments and penetration tests.
Coordinate documentation of security policies and procedures, and oversee testing of systems to ensure effective security measures are in place.
Analyze network data to determine usage and identify issues, and document findings from investigations of network evaluations.

Gather and analyze user feedback to locate or resolve sources of problems; provide technical assistance to computer users by answering questions or resolving computer problems for clients in person, via telephone, or electronically.

Document installation or configuration procedures to allow maintenance and repetition; document application and Web site changes or change procedures; document technical functions and specifications for new or proposed content management systems; and document design specifications, installation instructions, and other system-related information.

Perform network maintenance to ensure networks operate correctly with minimal interruption; monitor system operation to detect potential problems; test issues such as system integration, performance, and system security; and perform user testing or usage analyses to determine effectiveness or usability.

Identify, standardize, and communicate levels of access and security; perform risk assessments to develop response strategies; and implement Web site security measures, such as firewalls or message encryption.

Evaluate existing systems to determine effectiveness and suggest changes to meet organizational requirements; analyze, interpret, or disseminate system performance data; and monitor systems for intrusions or denial of service attacks, reporting security breaches to appropriate personnel.
Develop training materials for end users of document management and related systems; and train users in the operation and maintenance of systems.

Provide guidelines for implementing secure systems and collaborate with engineers or software developers to select appropriate design solutions or ensure compatibility of system components; communicate with staff or clients to understand specific system requirements and define and analyze objectives, scope, issues, or organizational impact of information systems.

Investigate system component suitability for specific purposes; evaluate or recommend server hardware or software; and check and analyze operating system or application log files regularly to verify proper system performance.

Perform ongoing hardware and software maintenance operations, including installing or upgrading hardware or software; ensure proper functioning of software patches and fixes; and implement updates, upgrades, and patches in a timely manner to limit loss of service.

Collaborate with development teams to discuss, analyze, or resolve usability issues; identify or address interoperability requirements; and develop or document standards and best practices for system usage procedures.
Correct testing-identified problems or recommend actions for their resolution; and monitor or track project milestones and deliverables.
Develop efficient and effective system controllers and establish functional or system standards to address operational requirements, quality requirements, and design constraints.

Gather website usage data and document website performance metrics; analyze or recommend website improvements, and develop budgets to support recommendations; and inform website users of problems, problem resolutions, or application changes and updates.

Install or configure web server software or hardware and ensure that directory structure is well-defined, logical, and secure, and that files are named properly; and develop testing routines and procedures for software packages for use in web operations or other applications.
Develop or implement procedures for ongoing web site revision; collaborate with web developers to create and operate internal and external websites, or manage projects, such as e-marketing campaigns.

Monitor web developments through continuing education, reading, or participation in professional conferences, workshops, or groups; assess current or future customer needs and priorities by communicating directly with customers or conducting surveys.

Develop system engineering, software engineering, system integration, or distributed system architectures; design and conduct hardware or software tests; and evaluate testing routines for adequacy, sufficiency, and effectiveness.

Prepare and record changes to official documents and confirm changes with legal and compliance management staff, including enterprise-wide records management staff; and administer document and system access rights and revision control to ensure security and integrity of master documents.
